name: The High Temple of the Body
descriptionSeed: A temple-city where the body is the whole practice — tiers of training-grounds rising a mountain, every soul honing breath, poise, and endurance as devotion. What rides with the body-pole here is LIFE and PEACE: this is not the martial Redline; it's serene, generative, a place of health raised to worship. And its flaw is its virtue's shadow — so devoted to the body that thought is neglected, and the Temple is slowly forgetting how to warn itself of dangers that aren't physical.
encounterFlavor: Devotion made physical — a master holding an impossible pose at dawn, a child taught to breathe before to speak, a healing that is pure Soma, a warm incuriosity about anything that can't be trained into the flesh.
questSeeds: The Temple faces a threat it can't punch — a rumor, a debt, a coming decision — and no one there has trained the mind to meet it; they need someone who thinks, and half of them resent needing it
A student's body has surpassed their teachers' and it's making them cruel; Soma without a mind to aim it is turning to arrogance, and the Temple doesn't have the vocabulary to name the problem
A Cogitant has come from across the Reach to 'save' the Temple from its ignorance and is making everything worse; bridging the two is the work

substrateSource.reason: Somatic discipline is a retuning of the body away from the lattice. Long practice thins the ground around it.

axisVectorNote: Derived from authored poleIntensity against world_node_atlas.axisOrder (SNG-180). Disposition — what this place IS. Distinct from world position.
worldPosNote: Deep expression of its Reach — placed near the equator on the somatic_reaches bearing. (SNG-180: authored position — WHERE it sits. Independent of axisVector, which is WHAT it is; a bastion is where the two disagree.)
